 {"id":"bd-17uj","title":"bd-ut02: distributed::recovery failure modes","description":"## Unit Tests for src/distributed/recovery.rs (1186 LOC, 16 existing tests)

Existing tests cover basic recovery paths. This bead targets UNTESTED failure modes:

### New Test Cases
- Recovery with duplicate symbols from same replica (ESI collision)
- Recovery with symbols from non-existent/unknown replica ID
- Insufficient symbols (K-1 symbols when K needed) — verify clear error
- Mixed source + repair symbols with exactly K total — boundary
- Symbol hash mismatch during verification — corruption detection
- Recovery cancellation mid-collection (cancel token fires during gather)
- RecoveryCollector ESI deduplication under concurrent insertions
- Recovery checkpoint: interrupted recovery resumes from partial state
- Collector metrics accuracy: verify counts match actual symbol flow
- Recovery with zero replicas configured — immediate failure

### Logging Requirements
Each test logs: replica count, symbols collected, ESIs seen, recovery outcome, timing.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 10+ new tests in recovery.rs #[cfg(test)]
- [ ] All failure modes return typed RecoveryError variants
- [ ] Cancel-safety verified (no resource leaks on cancel)","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:16:07.368377675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:03:40.167328114Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:54:47.019401457Z","close_reason":"done","deleted_at":"2026-02-03T00:03:40.167317875Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critical","distributed","unit-test"]}

 {"id":"bd-18mg","title":"WebSocket server/acceptor: upgrade handling and connection management","description":"# WebSocket Server/Acceptor Implementation

## Overview
Implements the WebSocket server/acceptor for inbound connections with full Cx integration,
structured concurrency, and cancel-correct client lifecycle management.

## Background & Motivation
Server-side WebSocket is essential for real-time applications: chat, notifications,
live dashboards, multiplayer games. Must handle many concurrent connections with
proper resource management and cancellation.

## Key Design Points

### WebSocket Acceptor
```rust
pub struct WebSocketAcceptor {
    config: WebSocketConfig,
}

impl WebSocketAcceptor {
    /// Accept a WebSocket upgrade from an HTTP request.
    /// The connection is owned by the region that calls accept().
    pub async fn accept(
        &self,
        cx: &Cx,
        req: &Request,
        stream: TcpStream,
    ) -> Outcome<WebSocket, WsError> {
        // 1. Validate upgrade headers (Connection: Upgrade, Upgrade: websocket)
        // 2. Validate Sec-WebSocket-Key
        // 3. Compute Sec-WebSocket-Accept
        // 4. Send 101 Switching Protocols response
        // 5. Return WebSocket in connected state
    }
}
```

### Connection Lifecycle
- Each accepted connection is owned by its accepting region
- Region cancellation propagates to all child WebSocket connections
- Connections send Close frame (1001) when region closes
- Server can broadcast close to all connections on shutdown

### Integration with HTTP Server
```rust
#[route(GET, "/ws")]
async fn websocket_handler(cx: &Cx, req: Request, stream: TcpStream) -> Response {
    let acceptor = WebSocketAcceptor::new(config);
    let ws = acceptor.accept(cx, &req, stream).await?;
    
    // Spawn WebSocket handler in current region
    cx.spawn(async move |cx| {
        while let Ok(msg) = ws.recv(&cx).await {
            // Handle message
        }
    });
    
    // Return upgrade response (already sent by acceptor)
    Response::switching_protocols()
}
```

### Graceful Shutdown
- Server signals shutdown to all connections via Cx cancellation
- Connections attempt Close handshake with timeout
- After timeout, forcibly drop connections
- No message loss for pending sends

## Dependencies
- Requires: WebSocket frame codec (bd-r4hu)
- Requires: WebSocket handshake (bd-1kg0)
- Blocks: WebSocket E2E tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] accept() validates and completes WebSocket handshake
- [ ] Connections owned by accepting region
- [ ] Cancellation triggers clean close with 1001
- [ ] Integration with HTTP server layer
- [ ] Subprotocol negotiation support
- [ ] Extension negotiation (permessage-deflate)
- [ ] Unit tests for accept/reject paths
- [ ] Integration tests with real HTTP server","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- `ws_server::tests::accept_valid_upgrade` - Accept valid WebSocket upgrade
- `ws_server::tests::reject_missing_upgrade_header` - Reject without Upgrade header
- `ws_server::tests::reject_invalid_key` - Reject invalid Sec-WebSocket-Key
- `ws_server::tests::compute_accept_key` - Verify Sec-WebSocket-Accept computation
- `ws_server::tests::subprotocol_negotiation` - Select subprotocol
- `ws_server::tests::extension_negotiation` - Handle extension negotiation
- `ws_server::tests::send_recv_messages` - Basic message exchange
- `ws_server::tests::close_initiated_by_server` - Server initiates close
- `ws_server::tests::close_initiated_by_client` - Handle client close

### Cancel-Correctness Tests
- `ws_server::cancel::cancel_during_accept` - Cancel during handshake
- `ws_server::cancel::cancel_active_connection` - Cancel with active connection
- `ws_server::cancel::region_close_all_connections` - Mass close on region close
- `ws_server::cancel::graceful_shutdown` - Ordered shutdown with timeout

### Integration Tests
- `ws_server::integration::accept_multiple_clients` - Multiple concurrent connections
- `ws_server::integration::broadcast_message` - Send to all clients
- `ws_server::integration::client_disconnect_handling` - Handle client drops
- `ws_server::integration::http_server_integration` - With HTTP layer

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Frame details, handshake headers
- DEBUG: Accept/reject decisions, message flow
- INFO: Connection lifecycle (accept, close)
- WARN: Invalid upgrade attempts, protocol violations
- ERROR: Accept failures with full context

### Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync::ws::server=debug,test=info"
cargo test -p asupersync ws_server:: -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee ws_server_tests.log
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:16:58.167942145Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T08:13:34.791662855Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T08:13:34.791560485Z","close_reason":"Implemented WebSocket server/acceptor with Cx integration: WebSocketAcceptor for upgrade validation, ServerWebSocket for server-side connections, accept()/reject() methods, protocol/extension negotiation. Tests blocked by scheduler errors.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","websocket"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-18mg","depends_on_id":"bd-1kg0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-18mg","depends_on_id":"bd-ku6n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:05Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-18mg","depends_on_id":"bd-r4hu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-197o","title":"[ALIEN][E2E] CI integration: run Phase 6 end-to-end suites deterministically","description":"## Goal

Wire the Phase 6 end-to-end suites into the standard testing workflow so regressions are caught automatically.

This is intentionally separate from unit tests:
- unit tests validate local invariants
- E2E validates cross-module integration + determinism + artifact stability

## What should run

- GEO e2e (bd-1i1w)
- HOMO e2e (bd-32n6)
- LYAP e2e (bd-zsoh)
- RAPTORQ e2e (bd-15c5)
- PLAN e2e (bd-3gqz)

## CI constraints

- Runs must be deterministic and bounded (no unbounded exploration).
- Output must be stable (prefer artifacts checked by hashes rather than timing).
- Detailed logs should be available in CI artifacts for debugging.

## Acceptance criteria

- A single command (documented in TESTING.md) runs all Phase 6 E2E suites locally.
- CI executes the same command and fails on:
  - invariant violations
  - non-deterministic outputs
  - golden checksum mismatches
- CI stores artifacts (reports + traces) for failed runs for postmortem.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CloudyReef","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:19:29.832727336Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T12:02:40.970717222Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T12:02:33.079127202Z","close_reason":"CI integration complete.  {"id":"bd-1sf","title":"Implement ObligationState and ObligationKind enums","description":"# ObligationState and ObligationKind Enums

## Purpose
These types define the two-phase effect system's tracking of linear resources. Obligations are tokens that MUST be resolved (committed or aborted) before their owning region closes.

## ObligationKind
```rust
enum ObligationKind {
    // Channel send permit (reserve capacity, then send)
    SendPermit,
    
    // Message acknowledgment (receive, process, then ack/nack)
    Ack,
    
    // Time-bounded resource lease (must renew or expire)
    Lease,
    
    // In-flight I/O operation (must complete or cancel)
    IoOp,
}
```

## ObligationState
```rust
enum ObligationState {
    // Obligation created, waiting for resolution
    Reserved,
    
    // Obligation fulfilled (effect took place)
    Committed,
    
    // Obligation cleanly cancelled (no effect)
    Aborted,
    
    // ERROR: Obligation lost (holder completed without resolving)
    Leaked,
}
```

## State Transitions

```
Reserved ──────► Committed
    │
    ├──────────► Aborted
    │
    └──────────► Leaked (error state, detected by runtime)
```

All three terminal states (Committed, Aborted, Leaked) are absorbing - once reached, no further transitions.

## Why These Obligation Kinds?

### SendPermit
Two-phase channel send prevents message loss under cancellation:
```rust
let permit = tx.reserve(cx).await?;  // Reserve capacity (cancel-safe)
permit.send(message);                 // Commit: actually send
// OR drop permit → Aborted (capacity released, no message lost)
```

### Ack
Two-phase receive with acknowledgment for reliable messaging:
```rust
let (item, ack) = rx.recv_with_ack(cx).await?;  // Receive but don't dequeue
process(item);
ack.commit();  // Dequeue permanently
// OR drop ack → Aborted (nack, message redelivered)
```

### Lease
Time-bounded resource ownership:
```rust
let lease = resource.lease(duration, cx).await?;
// Use resource...
lease.renew(duration);  // Extend lease
// OR lease expires → Aborted (resource released)
```

### IoOp
In-flight I/O operations bound to region:
```rust
let op = io.submit_read(buffer, cx).await?;  // Submit (region can't close yet)
let result = op.complete().await;             // Wait for completion
// OR op.cancel() → Aborted (I/O cancelled)
```

## ObligationState Semantics

### Reserved
- Obligation exists and must be resolved
- Blocks region close (quiescence requires zero reserved obligations)
- Shows up in obligation registry under holder's region

### Committed
- The effect took place successfully
- E.g., message was sent, ack was committed, I/O completed
- This is the "happy path" resolution

### Aborted
- The effect was cleanly cancelled
- No data loss, no side effects
- Capacity/resources released back
- This is the "safe cancellation" path

### Leaked
- ERROR STATE: Holder completed without resolving
- This indicates a bug in user code or library
- In lab mode: panic immediately
- In prod mode: log error, attempt recovery, continue
- Triggers futurelock detection

## Linear Resource Discipline

Obligations are LINEAR resources in the logic sense:
```
reserve : Cx → Obligation<K, 1>      // Introduce obligation
commit  : Obligation<K, 1> → Cx → () // Eliminate obligation
abort   : Obligation<K, 1> → Cx → () // Eliminate obligation
```

The `1` annotation means "exactly one use." Dropping without explicit resolution is detected and triggers Leaked.

## VASS/Petri Net View

For verification, obligations can be modeled as a vector addition system:
```
marking(r, k) = count of Reserved obligations of kind k in region r

reserve(k) → marking += 1
commit/abort(k) → marking -= 1
region_close requires marking = 0 for all kinds
```

## Implementation Requirements

1. **Both enums must be Copy, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq**
2. **ObligationKind should have Display** for tracing
3. **ObligationState::is_terminal()**: Returns true for Committed/Aborted/Leaked
4. **ObligationState::is_resolved()**: Returns true for Committed/Aborted (not Leaked)
5. **ObligationState::is_error()**: Returns true only for Leaked

## Drop Semantics

When an obligation token is dropped:
- If Reserved → transition to Aborted AND emit trace event
- If already terminal → no-op

In lab mode, dropping a Reserved obligation can optionally panic (configurable).

## Testing Requirements

1. All terminal states are absorbing
2. Leaked is only reachable from Reserved
3. Committed/Aborted are mutually exclusive endpoints
4. Obligation tracking correctly counts per-region

## Invariant Support

### INV-OBLIGATION-BOUNDED
```rust
∀o: O[o].state = Reserved ⟹
    T[O[o].holder].state ∈ {Running, CancelRequested, Cancelling, Finalizing}
```

### INV-OBLIGATION-LINEAR
```rust
∀o: O[o].state ∈ {Committed, Aborted, Leaked} is absorbing
```

### I2: Region close = quiescence
```rust
R[r].state = Closed(_) requires
    ∀o where O[o].region = r: O[o].state ≠ Reserved
```

## Example Usage

```rust
// Two-phase send
let permit: SendPermit<T> = tx.reserve(cx).await?;
// permit.obligation_state() == Reserved

if should_send {
    permit.send(value);  // → Committed
} else {
    drop(permit);  // → Aborted
}

// If cx is cancelled and permit dropped without explicit resolution:
// → Aborted (safe default)
```

## References
- asupersync_v4_formal_semantics.md §1.7 (Obligation States)
- asupersync_v4_formal_semantics.md §3.4 (Obligations transitions)
- asupersync_plan_v4.md §8 (Two-phase effects + linear obligations)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Defines `ObligationKind` and `ObligationState` per the spec (Reserved/Committed/Aborted/Leaked).
- States are absorbing after resolution (Committed/Aborted/Leaked).
- Implements deterministic, trace-friendly formatting.
- Unit tests cover state ordering (if any), absorbing behavior, and conversions used by the registry.

 {"id":"bd-1sx3","title":"Epic: Combinator Implementation (join!/race!/select!)","description":"# Epic: Combinator Implementation

## Overview

Implement the structured concurrency combinators that compose concurrent operations: join (wait for all), race (wait for first), select (wait for any with continuation), and related patterns.

## Background

Asupersync's combinators differ from typical async combinator libraries:
1. **Cancel-correct**: Losers in a race are properly cancelled and drained
2. **Two-phase aware**: Combinator results use Outcome, not Result
3. **Region-integrated**: Combinators operate within structured concurrency regions
4. **Obligation-safe**: Obligations are properly resolved across all branches

## Combinators to Implement

### join!
Wait for all futures, return tuple of results:
```rust
let (a, b, c) = join!(future_a, future_b, future_c).await;
```

Semantics:
- All futures run concurrently
- If any fails, others continue (collect all Outcomes)
- If any panics, others continue
- Final Outcome is the 'worst' (highest severity)

### race!
Wait for first to complete, cancel losers:
```rust
let winner: Either3<A, B, C> = race!(future_a, future_b, future_c).await;
```

Semantics:
- All futures run concurrently
- First to complete is the winner
- Losers are cancelled (request → drain → finalize)
- Wait for losers to fully drain before returning

### select!
Pattern-match on first completion with cancellation:
```rust
select! {
    a = future_a => handle_a(a),
    b = future_b => handle_b(b),
    else => handle_none(),
}
```

### timeout!
Race a future against a deadline:
```rust
let result = timeout!(Duration::from_secs(5), operation).await;
```

### try_join!
Like join but short-circuit on first error:
```rust
let (a, b, c) = try_join!(future_a, future_b, future_c).await?;
```

## Implementation Approach

1. **Macro-based**: Procedural macros for ergonomic syntax
2. **Type-safe**: Full type inference for all branches
3. **Cancelable**: All branches respond to cancellation
4. **Budget-aware**: Track poll counts across branches

## Dependencies

- Cancellation system must be complete
- Region tree must support concurrent children
- Obligation tracking for two-phase operations

## Success Criteria

- All combinators work correctly
- Losers properly drained in races
- Obligations resolved across all branches
- Type inference works without annotations
- Lab runtime tests deterministic
- Documentation with examples

## References

- asupersync_plan_v4.md Section 3.2: Concurrency Combinators
- Tokio join!/select! macros (for syntax reference)
- futures-rs FutureExt (for trait patterns)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T21:29:37.616024580Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T04:35:49.974785590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T04:35:49.974579898Z","close_reason":"All sub-tasks completed: join!, race!, select!, timeout! macros. Obligation leak detection dependency now resolved.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["combinators","core"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1sx3","depends_on_id":"bd-1r6o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1sx3","depends_on_id":"bd-3r4d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

-{"id":"bd-1tiq","title":"[AA][Sched] Work‑stealing with deterministic fallback","description":"## Goal
Design a multi‑queue or work‑stealing scheduler that preserves determinism when required.

## Why
We want throughput without sacrificing replayability. Deterministic mode must be preserved.

## Deliverables
- Work‑stealing design with deterministic fallback.
- Contention analysis + fairness metrics.
- Benchmarks for throughput scaling.

## Acceptance
- Throughput improves under multi‑core workloads.
- Deterministic mode remains bit‑for‑bit reproducible.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CloudyReef","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:08:16.838624341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T12:01:44.565745531Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tiq","depends_on_id":"bd-1iva","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:08:16.846409204Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tiq","depends_on_id":"bd-37l1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:11:56.992726573Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tiq","depends_on_id":"bd-3nod","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:11:57.056395116Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
